LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ELAINE WANG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THERE WAS MUTUAL CONSENT TO THE RELEASE. THE TEST FOR MUTUAL CONSENT IS "DETERMINED BY OBJECTIVE, RATHER THAN SUBJECTIVE CRITERIA, THE TEST BEING WHAT THE OUTWARD MANIFESTATIONS OF CONSENT WOULD LEAD A REASONABLE PERSON TO BELIEVE." (WEDDINGTON PRODUCTIONS INC. V. FLICK. (1998) 60 CAL.APP.4TH 793, 811.) HERE, THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF KNEW SHE WAS RELEASING ALL CLAIMS IN EXCHANGE FOR ONE MONTH OF SEVERANCE PAY OR WHETHER SHE THOUGHT SHE HAD AGREED TO NEGOTIATE HER SEVERANCE PACKAGE. (DISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS 8, 12, 13.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Apr-14-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLES J. WISCH, TEL. NO. 788-1945 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL D. BRUNO OF THE GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ELAINE WANG's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was mutual consent to the release. The test for mutual consent is "determined by objective, rather than subjective criteria, the test being what the outward manifestations of consent would lead a reasonable person to believe." (Weddington Productions Inc. v. Flick. (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 793, 811.) Here, the evidence shows that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff knew she was releasing all claims in exchange for one month of severance pay or whether she thought she had agreed to negotiate her severance package. (Disputed Material Facts 8, 12, 13.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-14-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHARLES J. WISCH, TEL. NO. 788-1945 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL D. BRUNO OF THE GORDON & REES LLP, TEL. NO. 986-5900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT REES SCIENTIFIC CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication is Denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether there was mutual consent to the release. The test for mutual consent is "determined by objective, rather than subjective criteria, the test being what the outward manifestations of consent would lead a reasonable person to believe." (Weddington Productions Inc. v. Flick. (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 793, 811.) Here, the evidence shows that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether a reasonable person would believe that Plaintiff thought she released all claims at the time she signed the document. Plaintiff's subsequent communications with Defendant's employees indicate that Plaintiff thought she had agreed to negotiate her severance package. (PSF 3.8, 6.1, 6.3, 6.5.) There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant had a legitimate, non-discriminatory reason for terminating Plaintiff's employment. Defendant met its burden by providing evidence showing that Plaintiff was terminated because she did not follow management's advice, she spent too much time in the office instead of with potential customers, and she failed to properly input the meetings she attended in the computer system. (UMF 38, 40.) Plaintiff's evidence shows that Defendant prioritized sales numbers, and her sales numbers were significantly better than the Caucasian male who replaced her. (PSF 35.1, 35.2, 35.5, 35.7, 35.8, 35.9, 40.1, 40.5, 47.1.) Plaintiff's evidence also shows that her performance as a sales representative was praised by her manager and she received an achievement award at the National Sales Meeting. (PSF 19.6.) Therefore, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff was terminated because of legitimate non-discriminatory reasons. Additionally, Plaintiff's evidence overcomes the same actor inference. "A strong inference is just that - an inference. The fact that a juror could reasonably draw a different inference is sufficient to preclude summary judgment." (Sandell v. Taylor-Listug, Inc. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 297, 324.) Here, given the evidence showing the emphasis Defendant put on sales numbers, as well as the evidence showing that Plaintiff significantly outperformed her replacement, a juror could reasonably draw a different inference as to why Plaintiff was terminated. Further, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Rick Saunders, a Regional Manager, initiated the process of Mr. Bussolini replacing Plaintiff because of her gender. (PSF 56.1-56.9.). ORDER SIGNED IN OEPN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
